'Ndrina Ierinò.description: The Ierinò or Jerinò are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Gioiosa Ionica, Calabria, Italy, involved in drug trafficking. They are also present in Ivrea, Salento, Borghesiana, and Pisticci. They are allied with the Mazzaferro, Cataldo, and Costa clans. The clan was involved in kidnappings in the 1970s and 1990s, including the 1975 kidnapping of Tobia Matarazzi and the 1993 killing of Vincenzo Mazzaferro.

Libertadores del Vichada.description: Colombian drug trafficking neo-paramilitary group involved in the Colombian armed conflict. Hezbollah.description: Hezbollah is a Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary organization founded in 1982 by Lebanese clerics in response to the Israeli invasion of Lebanon. It operates a social services network, runs Al-Manar TV, and holds seats in the Lebanese Parliament. Its paramilitary wing is considered equivalent to a medium-sized army. The group is part of the Axis of Resistance and has been banned by the Lebanese government since March 2026.

Chicago Outfit.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Chicago, Illinois, originating in the city's South Side in the early 1910s. Rose to power in the 1920s under Johnny Torrio and Al Capone during Prohibition. Involved in loansharking, illegal gambling, extortion, political corruption, and murder. Considered one of the most violent Mafia families in the U.S., with over 1,000 murders since the 1920s. Still active but in decline since the late 20th century.

Clan Lago.description: Camorra clan based in Pianura, a western suburb of Naples, Italy. Active primarily in the cement cycle, illegal building, extortion rackets, drug trafficking, and murders.
